channel.title: home on My Discombobulated Brain
channel.description: Recent content in home on My Discombobulated Brain

item.title: Corporate Live Wire Innovation and Excellence Awards 2024
Festivals
item.description:
At My Discombobulated Brain, we believe festivals should be spaces where everyone feels safe, supported, and free to enjoy the energy of the event. We’re here to make sure that mental health and well-being are prioritised, safeguarding your guests and providing the support they need to fully embrace your event.

  • Guidance and advice for organisers,
  • tips and support for attendees,
  • a register of mental-health-concious events.

item.title: Mental Health and Wellbeing Wales Awards 2023
Projects
item.description:
We work closely with organisations to be able to provide you with support, and them with advice. Whether it’s education, entertainment, or employment we want to ensure you can feel safe and supported in all parts of your day-to-day life.

  • Education and guidance for organisations,
  • help and support for users,
  • a flexible approach to working with all involved.

item.title: Resources
item.description:
We provide a range of useful resources that you can access right now to help you on your mental health journey, and help organisations provide you with the support you need.

  • Useful resources and toolkits,
  • Support in developing the tools you need,
  • A growing collection of resources

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Privacy Policy

Your privacy is important to us. This policy explains how we handle your data in accordance with UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018.

What information we collect

We may collect personal information such as your name, contact details, and any information you provide via our forms or services.

How we use your information

We use your information to provide our services, respond to enquiries, and improve our website. We do not sell your data.

item.title: Safeguarding
item.description:
Safeguarding

We are committed to safeguarding children, young people, and adults at risk. Our safeguarding policy outlines our approach and responsibilities.

Reporting concerns

If you have a safeguarding concern, contact us immediately. We take all reports seriously and act in accordance with UK law.

Last updated: 15 May 2026

item.title: Terms and Conditions
item.description:
Terms and Conditions

By using this website, you agree to comply with these terms. This site is operated by a UK mental health charity.

Use of site

Content is for information only and not a substitute for professional advice. Do not misuse the site or its content.

Limitation of liability

We are not liable for any loss or damage arising from use of this site.

Last updated: 15 May 2026

item.title: What to Expect from Counselling
item.description: A guide to the counselling process for first-timers.

item.title: Finding the Right Therapist
item.description: Tips for choosing a therapist who fits your needs.

item.title: Understanding Self-Harm
item.description: A compassionate look at why people self-harm and how to support them.

item.title: Donate
item.description:
We are a non-profit organisation, and donations help us continue the work we do every day.

Your support helps fund our resources, outreach, and community support for people who need compassionate mental health help.

item.title: Talking to Your GP About Mental Health
item.description: How to start a conversation with your doctor about your mental health.

item.title: Self-Care for Busy People
item.description: Quick self-care ideas for when life gets hectic.

item.title: The Power of Peer Support
item.description: How connecting with others who understand can make a difference.

item.title: Coping with Anxiety
item.description: Everyday strategies for managing anxious thoughts and feelings.

item.title: Workplace Wellbeing Tips
item.description: Simple ways to look after your mental health at work.

item.title: Understanding Panic Attacks
item.description: What panic attacks are, why they happen, and how to cope.

item.title: How to Support a Friend in Crisis
item.description: Practical steps for helping someone you care about during tough times.

item.title: Mental Health Myths Busted
item.description: Debunking common misconceptions about mental health and wellbeing.

item.title: About Us
item.description:
Our Story

We are a dedicated mental health charity committed to providing compassionate support, resources, and advocacy for individuals facing mental health challenges. Founded on the belief that mental health matters just as much as physical health, we work tirelessly to break down stigma and create a more supportive world.

You do not have to go through this on your own. Reaching out is a brave first step, and we are here to walk alongside you.

item.title: Talking About Mental Health at Work
item.description:
Talking about mental health at work remains difficult for many people. Fear of judgement, concerns about job security, and uncertainty about how a manager might respond all play a part. Yet the cost of staying silent, both to individuals and to organisations, is significant.

Why the silence persists

Many people have absorbed the message, often unspoken, that the workplace is not an appropriate place to discuss personal struggles. Professionalism, in many settings, has come to mean keeping difficult feelings out of sight. This is changing, but slowly.

item.title: Understanding Anxiety: What It Is and How to Cope
item.description:
Anxiety is one of the most common mental health experiences, affecting people in many different ways. For some it appears as a persistent hum of worry in the background of daily life. For others it can feel sudden and overwhelming. Neither experience is unusual, and neither means something is fundamentally wrong with you.

What anxiety actually is

At its core, anxiety is your body’s threat-response system doing what it was designed to do. When your brain perceives danger, it triggers a cascade of physical and mental changes, including a faster heartbeat, shallow breathing, and heightened alertness. In situations of genuine danger, this is genuinely useful.

item.title: Contact Us
item.description: If you’d like to get in touch, you can contact us by any of the methods listed here.
